MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT / Notice Of Application & Hearing For Right To Attach Order, Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Oct-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SCOTT D. GATTEY, Esq. of Gattey Law Office (Tel: 650-596-7123) for Plaintiff Linda Liu and Nick Yu;
Atty. CONSTANTINE "GUS" M. PANAGOTACOS, Esq. of Dunn & Panagotacos LLP
(Tel: 415-982-2100) for Defendant Mark Bright.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS LINDA LIU AND NICK YU's Application For Right To Attach Order, Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Linda Liu and Nick Yu's motion for writ of attachment is denied. As defendant Mark Bright points out, prejudgment attachment is a "harsh remedy," so facts favoring a writ must be "clear-cut" and requirements for writ issuance are strictly construed. (Martin v. Aboyan (1983) 148 Cal.App.3d 826, 831; Property Research Financial Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (1972) 23 Cal.App.3d 413, 422; Pacific Decision Sciences Corp. v. Sup Ct. (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 1100, 1106.) Given all that, plaintiffs' check-box forms and declarations laden with legal conclusions do not permit the findings required by Code of Civil Procedure 484.090.

Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed judicial council order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
